summary: Doc-only fixes to node.go/lookup.go confirmed logic-free via line-by-line diff review; additive restartFromRootCount instrumentation confirmed safe by construction and placement (single .Add(1) at each restart-continue branch, no control-flow/critical-section change). New test forces a real restart via direct latch hold + existing crabRetryHook, asserting counter strictly increases -- not a false positive. Full suite green with -race, new test stable across 5 repeated runs. Scope matches exactly: engine/btree/*, .cdr/memory/pending.md, .cdr/runs/2026-07-07/001-implementation/* only; engine/split/ untouched. pending.md diff removes exactly the 3 claimed items; other 2 pre-existing items plus the newer split.Trigger wiring item (concurrent task) remain unmodified.

requirements.evidence: All 3 pending.md items addressed as specified: node.go doc reword, lookup.go doc reword + rootMu exception documented, restartFromRootCount counter + accessor wired at all 3 call sites with a test.

architecture_conformance.evidence: No new synchronization primitives; counter increments happen outside any latch/lock critical section (crabInsert/crabDelete/Tree.Lookup's restart-continue branches are already outside per-node latch hold, matching publish-last discipline from 2a.4.5 fix).

regression_risk.evidence: go test ./btree/... -race -v -count=1 -timeout 10m: PASS, 59.97s, zero regressions, including TestCrabbingInsert, TestOptimisticRead, TestNodeLatchFields, TestPrefixScan* suites.

edge_cases.evidence: TestRestartFromRootCountIncrementsOnForcedRestart deterministically forces a restart via direct latch hold on the exact next-hop child, waits on existing crabRetryHook firing (bounded 2s), then asserts counter strictly increased -- rules out a vacuously-passing test.

security.evidence: No new attack surface; RestartFromRootCount() returns a value (uint64) via atomic.Load, no mutable state or pointer leak.

performance.evidence: Single atomic Add(1) per restart iteration; restarts are already the slow, rare path (root re-walk), so counter overhead is negligible and does not change retry timing/backoff.

maintainability.evidence: New doc comments are clear, cross-reference latch.go correctly, and the counter's own doc comment explains provenance (pending.md recommendation) and non-goals (not a cap).

test_coverage.evidence: New test read in full; reuses established crabRetryHook forced-contention pattern from insert_test.go. Ran -race -count=5 for the new test specifically: 5/5 PASS, no flakiness, ~0.04-0.08s each.

confidence.evidence: Diffs for node.go and lookup.go inspected line-by-line and confirmed comment-only outside the single counter line; insert.go/delete.go diffs are exactly one added line each at the correct branch; latch.go diff is purely additive (var + accessor).
